frame: Workplace: Menstrual and Maternity Leave Bias
generated_comment: Guys I'm so humiliated right now 😭 Rohit told sir in front of EVERYONE that I was absent because of 'women's problems' — like bhai, you couldn't just say I was unwell?? First time in my family koi corporate mein gayi hai and this is what happens, I want to disappear.
label: non_misogynistic
annotation_note: Aarti is the target of a workplace privacy violation and gendered embarrassment, not the perpetrator of misogyny. Her post documents how Rohit's casual disclosure of her menstrual absence weaponized her gender in a professional setting — a form of menstrual stigma and workplace bias directed at her, not expressed by her.
persona_text: You are Aarti. Age 21. A first generation college student from your family in Nagpur. You are the first woman in your family to get a corporate internship. You are posting anonymously in a college WhatsApp group after your male intern colleague told the supervisor that you were absent last week because of "women's problems" in front of the whole team. You are sharing what happened and how it felt different because of who said it and who you are.
